hop_context.sub_question: Where does spRefreshPrices source the ListPrice values? From external table or computed?

path_so_far.notes: TotalRevenue computed as Qty * UnitPrice (line 498). Two critical branches: Qty from vwConsolidatedSales, UnitPrice from vwPriceList. AdjustedRevenue uses TotalRevenue-Discount (secondary). Other neighbors (CustomerMaster, CustomerSegmentMap, RegionLookup) used only for context/filters, not revenue computation.
Simple view that renames OrderQty to Qty. DimCalendar used only for filtering (WHERE clause), not contributing to Qty value. SalesStaging is the actual source of OrderQty.
Multi-CTE view (3 renames: ListPrice?BasePrice?AdjPrice?UnitPrice). Pure transformation with 1.0 multiplier (no external source for adjustment). PriceMaster is the single source of ListPrice data.
Staging table with OrderQty. Two upstream procedures: spArchiveOldOrders (likely removes old rows) and spLoadSalesStaging (likely loads fresh data). Need to check both for OrderQty contribution.

path_so_far.summary: Source of ListPrice, which becomes UnitPrice through CTE transforms
path_so_far.notes: Source table for ListPrice (Qty is independent). PriceMaster is maintained by spRefreshPrices.

path_so_far.summary: Check if this procedure writes/modifies OrderQty in SalesStaging
path_so_far.notes: Reads OrderQty from SalesStaging and archives to ArchiveOrders, then deletes from SalesStaging. This is data maintenance/lifecycle, not source. Does not CREATE OrderQty. Cycle detected: reads what already exists.

path_so_far.summary: Check if this procedure writes/modifies OrderQty in SalesStaging
path_so_far.notes: TRUNCATE+INSERT loader. Selects OrderQty from vwRawOrders and inserts into SalesStaging. DimCalendar used only for UPDATE (DateKey backfill), not for OrderQty value. vwRawOrders is the actual source.

path_so_far.summary: Procedure that inserts/updates ListPrice in PriceMaster
Source view for OrderQty and other staging columns

focus_node.ct_ddl:
-- ─── spRefreshPrices (Branch B, Level 4b) ───────────────────────────────────
-- Loads PriceMaster from SupplierPrices + MarkupRules
-- CurrencyConfig is a FILTER-ONLY join (no columns flow to ListPrice)
CREATE PROCEDURE [ai].[spRefreshPrices]
AS
BEGIN
    SET NOCOUNT ON;
    -- Step 1: Load raw supplier costs into temp
    -- NOTE: CurrencyConfig is only used for filtering, NOT for price calculation
    SELECT
        sp.ProductCode,
        sp.CostPrice,
        sp.Currency,
        sp.EffectiveFrom,
        r.RegionCode
    INTO #SupplierCosts
    FROM [ai].[SupplierPrices] sp
    CROSS JOIN [ai].[RegionLookup] r
    -- CurrencyConfig: FILTER ONLY — controls which currencies we accept
    INNER JOIN [ai].[CurrencyConfig] cc
        ON sp.Currency = cc.Code
    WHERE cc.IsBase = 1
      AND sp.EffectiveTo IS NULL;
    -- Step 2: Apply markup rules to compute ListPrice
    -- TODO: also pull TotalRevenue from ai.LegacyFacts (table doesn't exist, old note)
    SELECT
        sc.ProductCode,
        sc.RegionCode,
        sc.CostPrice * (1 + COALESCE(m.MarkupPct, 0.15)) AS ListPrice,
        sc.EffectiveFrom
    INTO #PricedItems
    FROM #SupplierCosts sc
    LEFT JOIN [ai].[MarkupRules] m
        ON sc.RegionCode = m.RegionCode
       AND m.ValidTo IS NULL;
    /* Old approach: SELECT TotalRevenue FROM ai.SalesStaging
       This was the v1 logic that pulled prices from sales data.
       Removed in Q2 2025 when we switched to supplier-based pricing.
       SELECT s.OrderAmount AS ListPrice, s.RegionCode
       FROM ai.SalesStaging s
       WHERE s.IsValid = 1
    */
    -- Step 3: Upsert into PriceMaster
    MERGE [ai].[PriceMaster] AS tgt
    USING #PricedItems AS src
        ON  tgt.ProductCode   = src.ProductCode
        AND tgt.RegionCode    = src.RegionCode
        AND tgt.EffectiveFrom = src.EffectiveFrom
    WHEN MATCHED THEN
        UPDATE SET
            tgt.ListPrice     = src.ListPrice,
            tgt.LastRefreshed = SYSUTCDATETIME()
    WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN
        INSERT (ProductCode, RegionCode, ListPrice, EffectiveFrom, LastRefreshed)
        VALUES (src.ProductCode, src.RegionCode, src.ListPrice, src.EffectiveFrom, SYSUTCDATETIME());
    -- Cleanup
    DROP TABLE #SupplierCosts;
    DROP TABLE #PricedItems;
    EXEC [ai].[spLogAudit]
        @EventType  = 'REFRESH',
        @ObjectName = 'PriceMaster',
        @AffectedRows = @@ROWCOUNT;
END;
